task_completion.description: Conditions CSV file is created with the required word pairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named conditions.csv is visible in a text editor, spreadsheet application, or the PsychoPy loop handler, containing the pairs: BEES/HIVE, LION/ROAR, SOUP/BOWL, KING/QUEEN, TABLE/CHAIR.

task_completion.description: PsychoPy experiment is created and saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder window is open with 'paired_associates.psyexp' visible in the title bar or file path.

task_completion.description: Study routine is configured with a 2-second cue/target display.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A routine (e.g., 'study') is visible in the PsychoPy flow or routine tab, containing text components for the cue and target, with a duration (stop time) of 2.0 seconds visible in the component properties.

task_completion.description: Test routine is configured with an editable text box.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A routine (e.g., 'test') is visible in the PsychoPy flow, containing a Textbox component. The properties for this textbox should show it is configured as editable for participant input.

task_completion.description: Feedback routine is configured with case-insensitive comparison logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A routine (e.g., 'feedback') is visible containing a Code component. The code should show logic comparing the textbox input to the target (using methods like .lower() and .strip()), and setting feedback text to 'Correct!' (green) or 'Incorrect' (red).

task_completion.description: The experiment is run to verify functionality by completing at least one trial.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A PsychoPy experiment window is visible running the experiment, showing either the study display, the test display with text input, or the feedback display ('Correct!' or 'Incorrect').

integrity.description: Agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder interface with the Routine and Flow panels must be visible in the screenshots. The agent should not just write the .psyexp XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The experiment was executed genuinely using PsychoPy.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Runner window or a standard PsychoPy experiment display window is visible, indicating the experiment was actually run, rather than just printing text in a terminal.

integrity.description: Feedback logic dynamically compares input to the target.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code component for feedback uses variables (e.g., textbox.text and the target variable from the conditions file) rather than hardcoding the specific word pairs in the if/else statements.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Experiment path: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/paired_associates/paired_associates.psyexp
Conditions path: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/paired_associates/conditions.csv
Required pairs: BEES/HIVE, LION/ROAR, SOUP/BOWL, KING/QUEEN, TABLE/CHAIR
